title: eriastrum diffusum subsp. diffusum
intro: Eriastrum diffusum subsp. diffusum, also known as the Woodland lupine or Diffuse eriastrum, is a plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae) native to the southwestern United States. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Native American medicine, where it has been used to treat various ailments such as fever, colds, and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of Eriastrum diffusum subsp. diffusum are attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can provide relief from pain and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its health benefits and promote overall well-being.
